stories.title: How Orbán lost the Hungarian election
stories.summary: How Orbán lost the Hungarian election For two years, the Hungarian leader watched his support slip away. By MAX GRIERA in Budapest Illustration by Natália Delgado/ POLITICO Viktor Orbán likely knew what was coming. When U.S. Vice President JD Vance stood beside him last week and predicted the Hungarian prime minister would win reelection Sunday, Orbán gave a small wave of his hand as if to say, “I’m not so sure.” Ever since his challenger Péter Magyar burst onto the scene in early 2024, the Hung

stories.title: Magyar hints at ending Hungary’s block on €90B Ukraine loan
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — Hungary’s government will drop its opposition to the EU’s €90 billion loan to Ukraine, incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar signaled, bringing a four-month standoff to an end. Speaking at a press conference in Budapest, Magyar said the EU decision on the loan “was already made in December” and that the incoming Tisza administration “would like to be coherent” with previous commitments. Viktor Orbán, who lost Sunday’s general election by a land

stories.title: EU relieved to see back of Orbán but Magyar’s Hungary may still present problems
stories.summary: Even if migration policy and lukewarm support for Ukraine will raise tensions, Hungary will now be arguing for its own interests rather than Russia’s What Magyar’s victory means for EU and Hungary Europe live – latest updates In Brussels, the relief was palpable after the defeat of Viktor Orbán, the EU leader who recently declared himself at Vladimir Putin’s service . For the EU, Péter Magyar’s victory was all the sweeter, as voters decisively rejected Orbán’s fear-mongering campaign that sought

stories.title: Hungary's Magyar outlines policy in first news conference
stories.summary: Prospective prime minister Peter Magyar has promised to move Budapest back towards the European Union and away from Russia. However, he is likely to continue Viktor Orban's hardline policies on immigration.

stories.title: What Péter Magyar’s victory in Hungary means for Ukraine
stories.summary: KYIV — Ukraine has expressed relief at the political turn in Budapest, but the feeling may not last long. Despite Viktor Orbán’s landslide loss to Péter Magyar in Sunday’s Hungarian national election, officials in Kyiv are under no illusion that the two countries will suddenly become allies after years of worsening relations. Magyar’s victory represented a clear defeat for Moscow, which had backed Orbán for years, and was celebrated in Kyiv as a sign that the €90 billion in EU funds

stories.title: Will Orbán’s defeat in Hungary be a turning point for Europe? – The Latest
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule has come to an end after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won the Hungary election by a landslide. Orbán quickly conceded after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' result. His defeat will come as a blow to Donald Trump, who has repeatedly endorsed Orbán and whose vice-president, JD Vance, had travelled to Budapest days before the election. All eyes are now on Magyar to see how Hungary’s relationship with the EU and Nato could change after

stories.title: Hungarian election winner Magyar vows to rebuild EU relationship after stunning defeat of Viktor Orbán - Europe live
stories.summary: The Tisza leader said the electorate voted ‘not just for a change of government but for a change of the regime’ in Brussels The EU will start work with the new Hungarian government “as soon as possible” to make progress on issues including energy and the release of frozen European funds, the head of the European Commission has said. “We will start working with the government as soon as possible on the topics you mentioned and much more to make a swift and overdue progress to the benefit of the H

stories.title: Judge dismisses Trump’s lawsuit against Wall Street Journal and Murdoch
stories.summary: Judge rules complaint fails to outline malice after Trump argued lewd drawing allegedly sent to Epstein at heart of story was fake A Florida judge has dismissed a lawsuit filed last summer by Donald Trump over a Wall Street Journal report that he had sent a “bawdy” letter to the disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein in 2003, though the judge has given the US president two weeks to refile the case. Trump, who has had a habit of suing media companies inside and outside the White House, had argued th

stories.title: What does Péter Magyar’s win in Hungary mean for the EU and Ukraine?
stories.summary: Tisza leader has promised to repair his country’s ties with Brussels after years of strained relations Europe live – latest updates Péter Magyar’s resounding election victory brings an end to 16 years of rightwing populist rule under Viktor Orbán. But the effects of that win are likely to be felt well beyond Hungary’s borders, from Brussels to Moscow and from Kyiv to Washington. Continue reading...

stories.title: Orbán’s defeat holds lessons for US: ‘Autocrats may rise, but are not invincible’
stories.summary: Stunning loss of rightwing populist in Hungary carries symbolic significance for opponents of Donald Trump For US Democrats seeking rays of light in the dark landscape of Donald Trump’s authoritarian onslaught, illumination has arrived from the unlikely source of Budapest. Viktor Orbán’s stunning defeat in Hungary’s general election – ending 16 years of unbroken rule for his governing Fidesz party – carries symbolic and psychological significance for American politics out of all proportion to th

stories.title: The Guardian view on Hungary’s election: democracy reclaimed | Editorial
stories.summary: The defeat of Orbánism is a globally significant political moment. But it is, above all, a triumph for the citizens who mobilised to take their country back Prior to his landslide election victory in 2010, which was to lead to 16 unbroken years in power, Viktor Orbán would tell supporters: “We have only to win once, but then properly.” Achieving a so-called supermajority by winning two-thirds of parliamentary seats allowed Mr Orbán to change the constitution, and begin turning Hungary into

stories.title: Poland and South Korea upgrade ties to strategic defence partnership deal
stories.summary: During a visit to Seoul on Monday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung pledged to strengthen their cooperation, particularly in defence.

stories.title: 'We cannot ask any country to give up its territory,' Magyar says on Ukraine
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Viktor Orbán had largely centred his electoral campaign on Ukraine and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, blaming Kyiv for the Druzhba pipeline blockade to accusations of meddling in Hungarian elections. Will things change with Magyar in power?

stories.title: Von der Leyen uses Orbán defeat to push for end of veto in EU foreign policy
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen waited less than a day after Hungary voted Viktor Orbán out of office to call for the EU to get more power over national governments to force through foreign policy decisions. Governments should be able to push through EU policies ― on issues that could include sanctions on Russia and funds for Ukraine ― by majority rather than allowing individual nations to wield a veto, she told reporters in Brussels. Under Orbán, Hungary’s pr

stories.title: Viktor Orbán is gone. What does his fall mean for Europe? Our panel responds
stories.summary: Hungary’s return to democracy will be hard. But the impact of Péter Magyar’s decisive victory could be profound, inside the country and beyond Continue reading...

stories.title: Péter Magyar vows to pursue those who ‘plundered’ Hungary after election win
stories.summary: Prime minister-elect promises ‘new era’ for country after defeating far-right Viktor Orbán Hungary’s prime minister-elect, Péter Magyar, has pledged to pursue those who “plundered, looted, betrayed, indebted and ruined” his country, promising “a new era” after a landslide election victory over his far-right predecessor Viktor Orbán. Magyar, whose centre-right Tisza party won at least 138 of the 199 seats in parliament, said the full election results should be confirmed by 4 May and he hoped his

stories.title: Vance’s bad week: vice-president risks becoming face of two Trump foreign policy failures
stories.summary: Orbán is out in Hungary and talks have failed to end the war in Iran – ill-fated roadtrip has been setback for Maga aims Shortly before JD Vance’s ill-fated week crisscrossing the world, Donald Trump asked him during a private Easter brunch about how the Iran negotiations were shaping up. “If it doesn’t happen, I’m blaming JD Vance,” Trump said to laughs in the room. “If it does happen, I’m taking full credit.” The joke at Vance’s expense contained an unfortunate nugget of truth: this is not an

stories.title: ‘Now I’m hopeful’: Hungarians welcome the morning after 16 years of Orbán rule
stories.summary: Excitement and disbelief mix on the streets of Budapest after record turnout drives opposition’s landslide victory Péter Magyar vows to pursue those who ‘plundered’ Hungary, after election win In a small plaza facing Budapest’s Nyugati train station, Gabor, 40, confessed that he was very, very hungover. “I partied all night,” he said, laughing. “It was crazy, I thought Fidesz was going to win. Now I’m really hopeful.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 'painful' election result - video
stories.summary: Orbán concedes defeat after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' election result, less than three hours after polls closed on Sunday. 'I congratulated the victorious party,' the rightwing populist told supporters in Budapest after a landslide win by Péter Magyar’s Tisza party. 'We are going to serve the Hungarian nation and our homeland from opposition as well,' he said Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Continue reading...

stories.title: End of the Orban era: Hungary and Europe breathe again
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major change.

stories.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
stories.summary: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.title: Hungary election: Supporters of Peter Magyar's party celebrate in Budapest
stories.summary: Supporters of Peter Magyar, leader of the pro-European conservative TISZA party, celebrate on the banks on the river Danube in Budapest.

stories.title: Thousands celebrate in Hungary as Péter Magyar ends Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule – video
stories.summary: Thousands flooded the streets as Hungary's opposition leader, Péter Magyar, declared victory after his Tisza party claimed a landslide win over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party. The victory ends Orbán's 16-year rule and marks a significant shift for Hungary's relationship with the EU and Russia. Orbán was celebrated by conservatives across Europe and the US, but lost favour at home with voters who grew weary of economic stagnation, international isolation and oligarchs amassing wealth. His landslide

stories.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
stories.summary: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

stories.title: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
stories.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

stories.title: Hungary: jubilant scenes across Budapest follow Magyar’s victory
stories.summary: Hungary saw large crowds take to the streets on Sunday, 12 April 2026, after Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power.

stories.title: Rendszerváltó házibulivá változott Budapest belvárosa a Fidesz bukása után
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt eredményváróján is óriási volt a buli, de a legváratlanabb utcabál a Wesselényi utcánál zajlott, ahol az autók alig tudtak haladni. Ez nem nagyon zavarta őket: dudáltak, pacsiztak a bulizókkal, még a rendőrök is mosolyogva hajtottak el közöttük.

stories.title: Ez volt Trump reakciója Orbán választási vereségére
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnöknek a Fehér Ház udvarán szegezték neki a kérdést.

stories.title: Budapesten koncertezik Cate le Bon, aki producerként is nagyon emlékezetes lemezeken dolgozott
stories.summary: A walesi előadó a Magyar Zene Házában lép fel augusztusban.

stories.title: Szőlő utcai ügy: meghosszabbította Kovács-Buna Károly és még három gyanúsított letartóztatását a bíróság
stories.summary: A volt igazgató mellett börtönben marad egy volt nevelő, gyermekfelügyelő, és az egyik rendész is.

stories.title: A legszegényebb településeken most is a Fidesz tarolt
stories.summary: Kicsit kisebb lett a Fidesz-fölény, mint 2022-ben, de így is akadt olyan község, ahol 98 százalék felett teljesítettek a kormánypártok.

stories.title: A Kreml reméli, hogy folytatódik a „rendkívül pragmatikus” kapcsolat Magyarország és Oroszország között
stories.summary: „Magyarország döntött, és mi ezt a döntést tiszteletben tartjuk”, mondta Vlagyimir Putyin sajtótitkára hétfőn.

stories.title: Nagy-Britannia nem vesz részt az iráni tengeri blokádban
stories.summary: A brit kormány célja nem a blokád támogatása, hanem a Hormuzi-szoros mielőbbi megnyitása

stories.title: Orbán nélkül nincs Fidesz – élő elemzés Bíró-Nagy Andrással és László Róberttel
stories.summary: Élőben elemezzük a Tisza kétharmadának okait, miután a Fidesz nagyon kikapott. Mire elég, ha Orbán Viktor újjászervezi a pártot? Vendégeink: László Róbert és Bíró-Nagy András politikai elemzők.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin szerint furcsa, hogy Orbán az irgalmatlan uszító kampány után teljesen európai módra gratulált Magyarnak
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt politikus magabiztosan nyert a választókerületében, gratulált is neki a Fidesz jelöltje, szerinte teljesen korrekt volt.

stories.title: „Én ennyire nem vagyok szent” – a mandineres Mészáros Nóra nem gratulál Magyar Péternek
stories.summary: „Inkább nagyon jó munkát kívánok!”, írta a Facebookon a műsorvezető.

stories.title: Ursula von der Leyen: Amint lehet, elkezdünk együtt dolgozni az új magyar kormánnyal a magyar emberek érdekében
stories.summary: A befagyasztott uniós forrásokra is utalt ezzel az Európai Bizottság elnöke, aki azt mondta, „kivételes este volt a tegnapi”, Európa ma magyar, ez nem kérdés.

stories.title: Előkerült Szijjártó Péter
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter vasárnap nem volt ott a Fidesz eredményváróján, nem posztolt a közösségi médiában. Hétfőn Budapesten fotózták le.

stories.title: Grafikonokon mutatjuk a Tisza elsöprő győzelmét
stories.summary: A korábbi választási eredményekkel összehasonlítva látszik csak igazán, mekkorát nyert Magyar Péter pártja.

stories.title: Évekig kussban volt mindenki, most meg az egész ország üvölt
stories.summary: Euforikus hangulatban ünnepelte az Orbán-rendszer bukását a budai rakparton összegyűlt tömeg, sírva, táncolva, ölelkezve bulizták át magukat – szerintük már egy másik országba. Hogyan élték meg a választás napját és mit várnak a legjobban, ami a NER bukásával megszűnik, kérdeztük őket.

stories.title: Alig egy nappal a választás után elérhetetlenné vált a radnaimark.hu weboldal
stories.summary: A kormánypropaganda az oldalt Magyar Péter lejáratására akarta használni a választási kampányban.

stories.title: Magyar Péter elmondta, hogy nézett ki az Orbán Viktorral folytatott telefonbeszélgetése
stories.summary: Kollégánk megkérdezte, hogy nézett ki a vasárnap esti telefonhívás, amiben Orbán Viktor gratulált.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás szerdán találkozik a parlamentbe jutó pártok vezetőivel
stories.summary: Magyar Péter arra szólította fel Sulyokot, hogy minél hamarabb kérje fel a Tisza Pártot a kormányalakításra, és mondjon le.

stories.title: Az ukrán külügyminiszter azt mondta, Zelenszkij felvenné a kapcsolatot Magyar Péterrel
stories.summary: Andrij Szibiha szerint „helyes, európai jellegű hírek érkeztek” Magyarországról, ez új lehetőségeket nyit az orosz–ukrán kapcsolatokban.

stories.title: Szokatlan hangnemben válaszolt a MTVA, amikor a közmédia lehetséges megszűnéséről kérdeztük
stories.summary: „A közmédia a hatályos jogszabályok alapján köteles kiegyensúlyozott és sokoldalú tájékoztatást nyújtani”, írta az MTVA, miután egyszer sem hívták meg Magyar Pétert a választási kampányban.

stories.title: Az éhezők viadala színésze is Budapest utcáin bulizott a választás után
stories.summary: Josh Hutcherson éppen Magyarországon forgat. Ezért futott bele az Orbán tizenhat éves kormányzásának bukását ünneplő tömegbe a fővárosban.

stories.title: Fantasztikusan öregednek a Magyar Nemzet Tisza Párt és Magyar Péter bukását vizionáló cikkei
stories.summary: Annyit mondunk, hogy az utolsó koporsószöget Magyar Péter karrierjébe egy tucat alkalommal verte bele a kormánylap az elmúlt két évben.

stories.title: Magyar: We want to return Hungary to the fore of Central Europe
stories.summary: At his first post-election victory press briefing, Péter Magyar spoke about the first tasks his government will tackle, as well as how he will approach China, Russia, Ukraine, Hungary's neighbouring countries, and more.

stories.title: Magyar Péter lemondásra szólította fel több állami intézmény vezetőjét, megkérdeztük őket, mit gondolnak erről
stories.summary: Az állami szervek kitérő választ adtak, arra nem reagáltak, hogy lemondanak-e a vezetőik.

stories.title: Megszólalt Orbán Viktor
stories.summary: A Fideszben összehívják az aktivistákat és a jelölteket, április 28-án választmányi gyűlést tartanak majd.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nem jogállamellenes lépésekkel építjük majd újra a jogállamot
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a választások utáni első nemzetközi sajtótájékoztatóján elmondta, miről beszélne Putyinnal és Trumppal, és miért nem követeli Varga Mihály távozását az MNB éléről.

stories.title: Pócs János szerint a fiatalok azért szavaztak a Tiszára, mert új és buli
stories.summary: Tizenhat év után nem jutott be a parlamentbe, de szerinte semmit sem kellett volna a Fidesznek másként csinálnia. Magyar Pétert viszont emberszámba sem tudja venni.

stories.title: László Róbert: El tudom képzelni, hogy Putyin nagyon nagyot csalódott az európai lerakatában
stories.summary: A Political Capital választási szakértője és Bíró-Nagy András, a Policy Solutions vezetője arról is beszélgettek a Telex élő adásában, hogy van-e jövője Szijjártó Péternek.

stories.title: Vučić Magyar Péternek: Ki a keresztapa? Na, gyerünk, válaszolj! Ki a keresztapa?
stories.summary: Magyar azt mondta, hogy tudja „ki a keresztapa a magyar-szlovák-szerb barátság mögött.” Erre is reagált a szerb elnök, aki kitart Orbán mellett.

stories.title: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

stories.title: Record turnout expected as parliamentary election in Hungary gets under way
stories.summary: Polls opened at 6am across the country, and will close 7pm local time. So far, all figures point to a record turnout.

stories.title: Seismic shift: How 16 years of Hungarian politics changed in one day
stories.summary: The National Election Commission said voter turnout was at a record 77.8%, the highest ever recorded in a Hungarian election.

stories.title: Orbán and Magyar vote in high-stakes Hungarian election watched by EU
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán and Péter Magyar vote in a pivotal Hungarian election with record turnout expected, as the result could reshape Budapest’s role in the EU.

stories.title: An environmental disaster in Moldova has Russia’s fingerprints all over it | Paula Erizanu
stories.summary: The Ukraine war on our doorstep is a constant threat. Contaminated drinking water is a dangerous new twist In the second week of March, the nature vlogger Ilie Cojocari went out to film the arrival of spring on the Nistru (Dniester) river, 70 metres away from his home in Naslavcea, a village bordering Ukraine on the northernmost point of Moldova. But as he approached the river he could smell the stench of oil rising up from the water and see dark spots floating on its surface. Something was wron

stories.title: Hungary Election: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe
stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: Hungary Elections: There is hope that Peter Magyar can deliver a new beginning
stories.summary: What will Hungary’s elections bring? In an op-ed for Euronews, EU politician Valérie Hayer expresses hope that Péter Magyar could usher in a new political beginning, while warning that another term for Viktor Orbán risks cementing irreversible democratic backsliding.

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Tourists to Australia would have social media accounts vetted under Trumpian Coalition plan
stories.summary: In hardline rhetoric, leader Angus Taylor also says ‘many’ prospective migrants would be a ‘net drain’ on the country Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Coalition government would end Australia’s non-discriminatory immigration program and introduce Trump-style social media vetting for visa applicants, as Angus Taylor accuses Labor of allowing migrants of “subversive intent” into the country. As the opposition loses support to Pauline Hanson’s One Nation, Taylor says t

stories.title: ‘Deported or departed’: Georgia candidate targets immigrants in ad blitz
stories.summary: Republican gubernatorial candidate floods media with ads attacking immigrants, trans people and DEI before primary A campaign ad from Republican gubernatorial candidate Rick Jackson pledging that unauthorized immigrants committing violent crimes will end up “deported or departed” has inundated streaming services and social media in Georgia for weeks. “I don’t care if you’re a Muslim or a Mongolian, you don’t have the right to force your culture on our country,” the Jackson ad begins. “Too often,

stories.title: EU plans emergency state aid rule change to address soaring energy costs
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union will this month release a proposal to ease state aid rules as part of a suite of measures to help member countries deal with the energy crisis caused by the Iran war, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said Monday. It follows U.S. President Donald Trump’s declaration Sunday that the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after peace talks with Iran collapsed over the weekend. EU governments have been clamoring for changes to state aid rules a

stories.title: Trump threatens military action in Strait of Hormuz if Iran challenges new blockade
stories.summary: President Donald Trump threatened additional military action against Iran in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after a U.S. blockade in the critical waterway went into effect earlier Monday. In a post to Truth Social , the president said that the entire Iranian navy has been “obliterated,” with 158 ships laying at the bottom of the sea. But what hasn’t been attacked, he said, are “their small number of, what they call, ‘fast attack ships.’” While those ships are not currently considered a threat,

stories.title: Meloni slams ‘unacceptable’ Trump comments on Pope Leo
stories.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has condemned U.S. President Donald Trump’s social media tirade against Leo XIV, even as the president continued his war of words with the first American pope. Meloni — a Trump ally who backed the president for the Nobel Peace Prize — has recently distanced herself from the American leader following a bruising referendum defeat last month . “I find President Trump’s remarks about the Holy Father unacceptable,” Meloni said in a statement . “The Pope is the he

stories.title: Brazil’s former spy chief who fled country arrested by ICE agents in US
stories.summary: Alexandre Ramagem fled country after he was sentenced to 16 years for his role in plotting military coup in Brazil When Brazil ’s former president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to nearly 30 years in prison for an attempted coup, six other members of his cabinet were also found guilty and all began serving their sentences – except for one. Days before the verdict, Alexandre Ramagem, Bolsonaro’s former spy chief, fled by car to Guyana and boarded a flight to the United States, where he has remained

stories.title: Oil price dips below $100 a barrel after Trump claims Iran wants deal
stories.summary: US president’s previous threat to block strait of Hormuz had led to a 6.9% increase in earlier trading Explainer: strait of Hormuz blockade Oil prices have fallen back after briefly rising to above $100 a barrel as Donald Trump claimed Iran had made contact and wanted “very badly” to strike a deal in the face of his blockade of the strait of Hormuz. The Brent crude international benchmark rose above the key psychological threshold earlier in the day, at one point up 6.9% to $101.70 a barrel on n

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Donald Trump claims Iran ‘wants to work a deal’ and confirms blockade of strait of Hormuz

Lula looks set to face off against a senator almost half his age in what will be the leftist’s seventh presidential campaign since he first sought

stories.title: Israeli forces fire teargas at schoolchildren holding West Bank sit-in
stories.summary: Incident took place on first day back at school in small village, as settlers blocked pupils’ access Israeli forces have fired teargas at Palestinian schoolchildren who were staging a sit-in in the occupied West Bank after settlers blocked access to their school. stories.summary: A Balaton utóbbi nyakló nélküli beépítése is szerepet játszhatott abban, hogy a tóparti településeken alig találni olyat, amit a Fidesz nyert.

stories.title: Szabad választás, egyenlőtlen feltételek, elfogult közmédia – így látták az EBESZ megfigyelői a választást
stories.summary: A nemzetközi megfigyelők bírálták a Fidesz és az állam közötti határok elmosódását, de elismerték a választási szervek szakszerű munkáját.

stories.title: Elérhetetlenné vált Mészáros Lőrinc cégcsoportjának honlapja
stories.summary: A Mészáros Lőrinc családjának érdekeltségeit összefogó konglomerátumhoz közel 500 cég tartozik, a család vagyonát tavaly egybillió forint fölé becsülték.

stories.title: Fellobogóztatta a várost Pécs ellenzéki polgármestere, tekintettel a választás eredményeire
stories.summary: Hiszünk abban, hogy ezek a jelképek nem megosztanak, hanem összekötnek bennünket, közölte Péterffy Attila polgármester.

stories.title: Stánicz Péter: Nem számítok arra, hogy a köztársasági elnökünk visszaélne a jogköreivel
stories.summary: Az alkotmányjogász szerint Sulyok Tamás be fogja tartani a 30 napos határidőt, és össze fogja hívni az új Országgyűlést. Átmeneti időszakról, alkotmányozó többségről és a köztársasági elnök elmozdításának lehetőségéről is beszélt a Telex adásában.

stories.title: Mentővel karambolozott egy furgon a XIII. kerületben, a mentőautó fel is borult
stories.summary: A Hajdú utca és a Fáy utca kereszteződésénél történt a baleset.

stories.title: Karácsony az elszámoltatásról: Úgy nem lesz ebben az országban béke, hogyha nem teszünk ebben igazságot
stories.summary: A főpolgármester a Telex élő műsorában azt mondta a Tisza kétharmadáról, hogy ez óriási lehetőség, de nyilván van benne kockázat.

stories.title: Róna Dániel szerint még három mandátumot szerezhet meg a Tisza
stories.summary: A 21 Kutatóközpont elemzője arról is beszélt a Telex élő műsorában, hogy a Fideszen belül már bőven a választás előtt nagy vereségre készülhettek.

stories.title: Igazságügyi szakértő rekonstruálta, hogyan zuhanhatott le az autó a Váci úti felüljáróról
stories.summary: Legalább 130 km/órával mehetett az Audi, amikor a vétlen Toyotának ütközött. A közlekedési ügyvédet az ámokfutók elleni jogi lehetőségekről, a pszichológust pedig arról kérdeztük, tisztában vannak-e ezek a sofőrök azzal, hogy mások életét veszélyeztetik.

stories.title: Családi békével ért véget az ország legnagyobb molinóháborúja
stories.summary: A balatonfüredi ház országos hírűvé vált erkélypárosán egy tiszás és egy fideszes üzenet feleselt egymásnak. A választások után azonban a Tiszát támogató Sándor szerint az a lényeg, hogy a politika ne verjen éket barátok, családtagok közé.

stories.title: Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire
stories.summary: PM tells Guardian Australia Hezbollah should cease reprisals and confirms Australia’s military surveillance aircraft will remain in region Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s prime minister, Anthony Albanese, has urged Israel to stop its attacks on Lebanon and raised concern over its intensified military campaign on Beirut and the country’s south after the ceasefire in the Middle East. Albanese also calle

stories.title: Nigerian airstrike targeting jihadists reportedly kills at least 100 civilians
stories.summary: Officials confirm misfire as Amnesty gives death toll after speaking to survivors of strike on market in Yobe state A Nigerian air force strike targeting jihadist rebels hit a market in north-east Nigeria, killing more than 100 people and injuring many others, Amnesty International and local media have said. Officials confirmed a misfire had occurred but did not provide details. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
stories.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

stories.title: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
stories.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching 36-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: According to Ukraine's General Staff of the armed forces, by 7am the ceasefire had been violated by Russia 2,299 times, including assaults, shelling and small drone launches. However, Russia has refrained from using long-range drones, missiles or guided bombs.

stories.title: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island
stories.summary: Cyclone crossed coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells Cyclone Vaianu made landfall in New Zealand’s North Island on Sunday, triggering floods, power outages and forcing hundreds to evacuate . The cyclone crossed the coast near the Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, national weather provider MetService said, describing Vaianu as a “life-threatening” system. Co

stories.title: Planeloads of negotiators and too little time: US and Iran’s 21 hours of talks
stories.summary: The two sides turned up to test one another’s resolve. It was probably unrealistic to expect a dispute that has taken up years of discussion to be settled in one marathon session Middle East crisis – live updates It was as if the two delegations in the Iran-US peace talks in Islamabad hoped that the sheer number of negotiators flown into Pakistan could overcome the handicap of having only a finite number of hours in which to settle a 20-year dispute over Iran’s nuclear ambitions, now overlaid by

stories.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
stories.summary: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

stories.title: Boosting women's role in politics, Dutch-style
stories.summary: Some 504 women were elected in recent Dutch local elections, with a "Vote for a Woman" campaign boosting their chances to gather preferential votes. Despite the record figure, women remain underrepresented.

stories.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
stories.summary: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.summary: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Trump threatens US 'blockade' of Strait of Hormuz after Iran ceasefire talks in Pakistan fail
stories.summary: Trump said in a social media post he has “instructed our Navy to seek and interdict every vessel in International Waters that has paid a toll to Iran. No one who pays an illegal toll will have safe passage on the high seas”.

stories.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
stories.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

stories.title: 'We need real peace': Ukrainians have little faith in Easter truce
stories.summary: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

stories.title: Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran talks fail in Islamabad
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran in Islamabad ended without a deal. The move ramps up pressure as both sides blame each other, while Pakistan urges restraint and the ceasefire hangs in the balance.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV urges immediate ceasefire as Israel-Hezbollah clashes rage
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV called for an immediate ceasefire on Sunday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues, urging all parties to respect international law and protect civilians.

stories.title: Whale Timmy: Can whale songs still save him? Biologist prepares autopsy
stories.summary: For days now, marine biologists have been assuming that there is no rescue for Timmy the humpback whale stranded in the Bay of Wismar. The Environment Minister of Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania is now talking about a "minimally invasive attempt to mobilise the whale".

stories.title: UK starts crackdown on EU citizens’ post-Brexit rights
stories.summary: Concerns raised over use of travel data in determining if people are ‘continuously’ in Britain after HMRC fiasco UK ministers are to start removing post-Brexit residency rights from EU citizens who are no longer “continuously” living in the country. The initiative is legal under the 2020 Brexit withdrawal agreement, but the decision to use travel data to partly determine absences has raised concerns after the HMRC fiasco in which almost 20,000 parents were stripped of child benefits because of i

stories.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
stories.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.
